specific_mechanism: Shear-thinning fluids have apparent viscosity that decreases with shear rate (e.g., Carreau, Cross models); below a critical yield stress they behave nearly rigidly, above it they flow with reducing resistance. The structural origin is shear-induced reorganization of microstructure (polymer disentanglement, particle alignment); the constitutive relation is non-linear in shear rate
llm_application: Model an LLM activation pathway as a shear-thinning channel: at low 'shear rate' (low input magnitude or rare-token contexts) the pathway is high-viscosity (rigid, slow to update); at high 'shear rate' (high-magnitude, common-token contexts) the pathway becomes low-viscosity (rapid update, sensitive to perturbation). Use the rheological constitutive relation to predict the activation's behavior under varying input intensity.
novelty_claim: Existing activation analysis treats them as linear features. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work imports the shear-thinning constitutive relation (apparent viscosity = f(shear rate)) as a model of activation-pathway state-dependent updatability.
